sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: The upcoming clash between Genoa and Milan is set for May 5, 2025, at 18:45 at the iconic Luigi Ferraris Stadium in Genoa. This Round 35 encounter in the Serie A promises to be intriguing as 13th-placed Genoa, with 39 points, host 9th-placed Milan, who have amassed 54 points so far. With both teams in different halves of the table, Milan will be looking to solidify their standing, while Genoa aim to climb higher.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Historically, Milan have had the upper hand in recent meetings against Genoa, with 3 wins in the last 5 matchups compared to Genoa’s 0 wins and 2 draws. Their most recent encounter on December 15, 2024, ended in a goalless 0-0 draw, where Milan’s Emerson Royal earned the Player of the Match title with an impressive Sofascore rating of 8.3. Head-to-head trends also indicate low-scoring games, with 4 out of the last 5 matches having less than 2.5 goals.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Genoa are currently struggling, riding a streak of 3 matches without a win and failing to score in those games as well. Their last 5 home games have consistently seen less than 2.5 goals, suggesting a defensive or low-scoring trend at Luigi Ferraris. Milan, on the other hand, have shown a knack for high corner counts on the road with 5 out of their last 6 away games featuring more than 10.5 corners, though their away matches often see less than 4.5 yellow cards in 6 out of 7 games.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Genoa
sections.text: For Genoa, Aarón Martín stands out with 57 key passes, ranking 3rd in the league, and 4 assists, showcasing his playmaking ability from midfield with a rating of 7.07. Defender Mattia Bani is also noteworthy, boasting a league 2nd rank in ground duels won at 73.7%, making him a defensive rock with a rating of 6.82. Up front, Andrea Pinamonti leads the attack with 8 goals, overperforming his expected goals of 7.37, while Johan Vásquez adds defensive solidity with a team-high rating of 7.06.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Milan
sections.text: Milan’s attack is powered by Christian Pulišić, who ranks 1st in the league with 61 key passes and is joint 2nd with 8 assists, alongside a 7.24 rating and 10 goals. Rafael Leão is another star, with 8 assists (also 2nd in the league) and 7 goals, ranking 3rd for successful dribbles per game at 1.8, complemented by a 7.2 rating. Midfielder Tijjani Reijnders has also shone with 10 goals and 4 assists, while defender Theo Hernández adds flair with 4 goals and a matching 7.2 rating.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Statistics
sections.text: Genoa’s season stats reveal a strong defensive setup, ranking 1st in tackles (18 per game) and interceptions (8.09 per game), though their attack struggles, scoring only 29 goals (16th in the league). They overperform their expected goals by 5.13, ranking 3rd in goals vs expected goals difference, with 72.4% of their goals assisted (6th in the league). Milan, meanwhile, score heavily in the 61-75 minute period (13 goals) and early in games (6 goals in the first 15 minutes), which could test Genoa’s defense late in halves.

sections.subtitle: Disciplinary and Set-Piece Expectations
sections.text: The referee for this match, Giuseppe Collu, averages 4.6 yellow cards per game and 0.13 red cards, hinting at a potentially feisty encounter. Genoa commit fouls frequently, with 436 fouls this season (11th most in the league), and have 66 yellow cards. Milan’s away games often see fewer cards, while Genoa’s home matches trend towards fewer than 10.5 corners in 7 out of 8 games, contrasting with Milan’s high corner count on the road.

sections.subtitle: Missing Players Impact
sections.text: Genoa will miss key midfielder Fabio Miretti, who has 3 goals and 3 assists, alongside forward Caleb Ekuban with 1 goal and 3 assists, potentially weakening their midfield and attack. Milan are without defender Emerson Royal, who impressed in the last matchup with an 8.3 rating, which might affect their defensive balance. These absences could tilt the tactical battle, especially for Genoa, who are already on a poor run.
